title: Volumes, PersistentVolumes, Claims, StorageClasses, CSI, Snapshots, and Recovery · API Object Lens: K8s Csi Snapshot Migration
summary: A stateful intake service binds storage in the wrong zone, deletes data through a reclaim policy, and treats a crash-consistent snapshot as a proven application restore. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s csi snapshot migration increment in stateful storage and recovery contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain CSI controller and node roles, drivers, snapshots, clones, expansion, topology, credentials, feature support, and deprecated in-tree migration boundaries.
Design StorageClasses with provisioner, parameters, defaulting, reclaim policy, expansion, mount options, and WaitForFirstConsumer topology behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Explain CSI controller and node roles, drivers, snapshots, clones, expansion, topology, credentials, feature support, and deprecated in-tree migration boundaries. A defensible solution applies K8s Csi Snapshot Migration, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to stateful storage and recovery contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-csi-snapshot-migration — Explain CSI controller and node roles, drivers, snapshots, clones, expansion, topology, credentials, feature support, and deprecated in-tree migration boundaries.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-storage-persistence-recovery-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-storage-persistence-recovery-3
    artifact: storage-recovery.yaml
    decision: worked k8s storage persistence recovery 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      claim: intake-data
      storageClass: retained-zonal
      volumeBindingMode: WaitForFirstConsumer
      reclaimPolicy: Retain
      snapshot: application-quiesced
      restore: fresh-namespace-and-claim
      checksum: verified
    verify: kubectl get pv,pvc,storageclass,volumesnapshot -A -o json
    failure: Reject Bound as durability proof, Delete reclaim surprises, arbitrary-zone binding, snapshot-as-backup claims, or restore without application validation.
    repair: Trace claim and volume lifecycle, align topology and access, quiesce the application, protect metadata and keys, and rehearse clean restore.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

# Evidence: k8s-csi-snapshot-migration-12o1bm

    artifact: storage-recovery.yaml

    environment: kubeadm recovery cluster restored onto replacement nodes
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: Pod state previous exit endpoint readiness and request result

    failure: Reject Bound as durability proof, Delete reclaim surprises, arbitrary-zone binding, snapshot-as-backup claims, or restore without application validation.
